title: Trump's proposed NASA budget is a 'horrible threat to our future' in space, Planetary Society CEO says
summary: The Planetary Society CEO criticized Trump's proposed NASA budget as a significant threat to future space exploration. The organization is advocating to restore NASA's funding by 2027.

title: James Webb Space Telescope directly studies an exoplanet's surface for the 1st time: 'We see a dark, hot, barren rock'
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to directly study the surface of a distant super-Earth exoplanet, describing it as a dark, hot, and barren rock. This marks the first time the telescope has analyzed an exoplanet's surface composition.

title: Watch the Eta Aquarid meteor shower online with these free livestreams
summary: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower can be observed live through free online livestreams from global observatories and sky cams.

title: Are streamers losing faith in legacy sci-fi franchises?
summary: The cancellation of 'Starfleet Academy' raises concerns about the future of 'Star Trek' and 'Star Wars' franchises. Streamers appear to be losing confidence in legacy sci-fi content.

title: Will the moon ruin the Eta Aquarid meteor shower this week? Here's what to know
summary: A bright waning gibbous moon may interfere with the peak of the Eta Aquarid meteor shower this week, though early risers might still spot a few shooting stars.

title: Our Milky Way's 'Zone of Avoidance' holds a galaxy supercluster with 30,000 trillion times the sun's mass
summary: Astronomers have discovered the Vela Supercluster in the Milky Way's 'Zone of Avoidance,' containing 30,000 trillion times the sun's mass. This massive structure is competing gravitationally with other superclusters for the attention of nearby galaxies.

title: Lasers take aim at a galaxy far, far away | Space photo of the day for May 4, 2026
summary: The ESO's Very Large Telescope Interferometer (VLTI) is using four lasers to study the Tarantula Nebula, creating a scene reminiscent of 'Star Wars'. The image, featured as Space Photo of the Day for May 4, 2026, highlights advanced astronomical technology.

title: What's the best time to see Eta Aquarid meteors in 2026?
summary: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower is peaking this week, but visibility depends on local conditions. The event is a recurring astronomical phenomenon.

title: A YouTuber just recreated the original Star Wars with cardboard, and it's awesome (video)
summary: YouTuber Zach King recreated the original Star Wars using cardboard, showcasing creativity and fandom. The video has been praised for its ingenuity and homage to the 1977 film.

title: 13 International 'Star Wars' Posters You've Likely Never Seen
summary: A collection of 13 unique 'Star Wars' posters from various countries, including Japan and Turkey, is being celebrated for Star Wars Day. The posters feature artwork inspired by the original trilogy.

title: NASA Welcomes Ireland as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Ireland signed the Artemis Accords on Monday, becoming the latest nation to commit to responsible space exploration. The signing ceremony was hosted by NASA, highlighting Ireland's role as a valued international partner.

title: NASA Fosters Development of Lunar Resource-Seeking Technologies
summary: NASA is collaborating with industry partners to develop technologies for extracting lunar resources like hydrogen and helium-3 from regolith, enabling long-duration missions to the Moon and Mars through in-situ resource utilization (ISRU).

title: NASA Welcomes Malta as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Malta became the 65th signatory to the Artemis Accords, joining NASA and the U.S. Department of State in a ceremony held in Kalkara. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Malta to the Artemis Accords community.

title: Breaking Barriers at 3rd Annual Findings from the Field Symposium
summary: The 3rd Annual Findings from the Field Symposium showcased student research with 106 participants and 68 research posters. The event included educators, experts, and various interactive sessions.

title: Blue Origin Moon Lander Completes Testing at NASA Vacuum Chamber
summary: Blue Origin's uncrewed cargo lander, Endurance (MK1), completed testing in NASA's vacuum chamber as part of its commercial demonstration mission for the Artemis program. The tests were conducted under a reimbursable Space Act Agreement, showcasing a public-private partnership.

title: Hubble Spots a Starry Spiral
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the spiral galaxy NGC 3137, located 53 million light-years away in the constellation Antlia. This galaxy provides astronomers with a prime opportunity to study the lifecycle of stars.

title: For NASA’s TESS, Stellar Eclipses Shed Light on Possible New Worlds
summary: NASA's TESS mission used data on stellar eclipses to identify over two dozen candidate exoplanets, expanding its detection capabilities. To date, TESS has confirmed 885 exoplanets and identified over 7,900 candidates.

title: LAGEOS: An Earth Science Mission Built for Enduring Precision
summary: On May 4, 1976, NASA launched LAGEOS, a unique Earth science satellite resembling a disco ball, into orbit to study Earth's geodynamics using laser reflectors. The mission remains a landmark in precision space-based measurements.

title: Record-Setting Retreat of Hektoria Glacier
summary: Scientists used satellite data to analyze the rapid ice loss of the Hektoria Glacier in Antarctica. This retreat is noted as record-setting in terms of speed and scale.

title: NASA Kennedy Center Director Announces Plans to Retire
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center Director Janet Petro announced her retirement after a career spanning military, industry, and space agency roles, including her tenure at NASA since 2007.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 flight, potentially marking a calm period before increased activity in space launches.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming mission aims to build on previous test flights with improved performance and safety measures.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday, marking a significant milestone in the country's spaceflight capabilities.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor, experienced in mass casualty events and overseas service, is set to discuss their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut's background highlights their readiness for the challenges of spaceflight.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Arianespace's Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. The launches will take place in Florida, California, and French Guiana.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were highlighted in a report by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission two weeks prior.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones in space exploration and science.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving parachute deployment for a space mission, emphasizing the importance of thorough testing in extreme conditions. It highlights the challenges and meticulous preparation required for successful parachute operations during re-entry.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session and an in-person event in Paris. The forum aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully transmitted from an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the project.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Lt. Gen. Schiess Nominated to Lead Space Force
summary: Lt. Gen. Schiess has been nominated to lead the U.S. Space Force, potentially becoming its third chief of space operations since the branch's establishment in 2019.

title: Payload Field Guide: Lunar Rovers
summary: NASA and its partners are advancing lunar rover technology to support sustained human presence on the Moon, moving beyond Apollo-era mobility solutions. Modern lunar rovers are being developed to handle long-term surface operations and heavier payloads.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: Space nuclear industry executives are optimistic about NASA's FY27 budget proposal, which Administrator Isaacman views as a key step in the agency's evolution. The proposal signals strong support for nuclear space technologies.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: A House subcommittee has approved a $24.4 billion NASA budget, rejecting the administration's FY27 request. Lawmakers expressed disapproval of the proposed spending plan during the past week.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space firms secured €1.4 billion in private funding in 2025, reflecting growing investor confidence in the sector's upstarts.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has raised £9.8 million in seed funding to manufacture cancer drugs in orbit using microgravity. The company aims to reformulate drugs for self-administration by patients.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has unveiled a new satellite product designed for urban planning and risk assessment. The tool aims to provide construction firms, insurers, and city authorities with real-time data on urban changes and risks.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and multi-domain military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance York's market presence.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The competitive landscape for orbital data centers includes major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Sophia Space, along with satellite suppliers such as Apex and K2 Space.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit AI processing by hosting Liquid AI's large language model to analyze an Earth observation image directly in space. The mission, Clustergate-2, launched on a Momentus Vigoride 7 satellite in March.

title: NRO Makes New Commercial Awards to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded new contracts for Earth sensing data to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel under its Strategic Commercial Enhancements (SCE) effort. This expands the NRO's commercial satellite data partnerships.

title: STMicroelectronics Expects its Space Business to Reach More than $3B Over Three Years
summary: STMicroelectronics, a global semiconductor company supplying SpaceX and other space firms, expects its space business to exceed $3 billion in revenue over the next three years.

title: Lockheed Martin Joins Seagate Space and Firefly Aerospace to Develop Sea-Based Launch
summary: Lockheed Martin has partnered with Seagate Space and Firefly Aerospace to develop a sea-based launch platform. The collaboration aims to enhance offshore launch capabilities for space missions.

title: Future-Proofing Satellite Networks With ST Engineering iDirect Sales SVP Brian Jakins
summary: ST Engineering iDirect discusses strategies to future-proof satellite networks amid advancements like 5G. The article highlights the role of technology in ensuring network resilience and scalability.

title: Vantor Nabs $70 Million NGA Award For GEOINT Portal Use
summary: Vantor secured a $70 million award from the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) for the first option year of a program supporting GEOINT portal usage. The contract highlights Vantor's role in providing geospatial intelligence solutions to the agency.

title: Lynk, EarthDaily, True Anomaly Launch Satellites on Weekend SpaceX Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX launched 45 payloads, including satellites from Lynk, EarthDaily, and True Anomaly, aboard a rideshare mission. The primary payload was a South Korean Earth Observation satellite.

title: Loft to Build 10 EO Satellites for French Constellation With Magellium Artal Group
summary: Loft will build 10 Earth Observation (EO) satellites for a French constellation in collaboration with Magellium Artal Group. This partnership marks a significant step in France's next-generation EO satellite initiatives.

title: Orbital Warfare Systems Top Priority for Space Chief Nominee Lt. Gen. Schiess
summary: Orbital warfare systems have been designated as the top priority by the White House’s nominee to lead the U.S. Space Force. Lt. Gen. Stephen Schiess Fielding aims to succeed Gen. B. Chance Saltzman in this role.

title: Sateliot and Telefónica España to Explore 5G Satellite Connectivity for Defense, Military Uses
summary: Sateliot and Telefónica España have partnered to explore 5G satellite connectivity for defense, military, and remote area applications using Sateliot's satellite IoT technology.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discussed tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions at Modern Day Marine to enhance network resiliency. The conversation highlighted Lite Coms' product designs for robust communication capabilities.
